A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YING Q. SUNG VS. GARY NELSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 25, 2021 line 3 (part one). DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT (PART ONE) is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Based on the allegations in the Complaint the premises have been uninhabitable for 30 years (e.g. Complaint paragraphs 12 and 18). Further based on the allegations in the complaint the premises were in good condition at the inception of the Lease as of the time Plaintiff took possession (See Lease, Ex. A to Complaint). Complaint contains no facts showing that Plaintiff notified the landlord of the condition of the premises of which Plaintiff had exclusive possession prior to March 20, 2019 via complaint to the DBI. Complaint does not provide any facts and opposition provides no authority for the proposition that the landlord had any obligation or right to inspect the premises of which Plaintiff had exclusive possession and provided no notice of the conditions thereof. Based on the allegations in the complaint claims based on the habitability alone are barred based on any possible statute of limitations. Several causes of action, however, are not premises solely on habitability issues. Therefore: 1. Demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is based solely on failure to provide Plaintiff with habitable rental premises. Even if the 1-year statute of limitations started running on March 20, 2019, it has run on March 20, 2020. 2. Demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of the conclusion that the repairs have not been properly done when undertaken, including the time of the repairs and the nature of the violation, 3. Demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. This cause of action is based in part on May 13, 2019 Notice. The 1-year statute of limitations has not run at the time of the filing of the complaint. Emergency Rule 9. 4. Demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to the extent this cause of action is based in service of May 13, 2019 Notice. Plaintiff to allege facts in support of cause of action for discrimination, which is the predicate for SFRO 37.10(a)(9). 5. Demurrer to the fifth cause of action is OVERRULED, all elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged and are not time barred arising out of May 19, 2019 notice. 6. Demurrer to the sixth cause of action is OVERRULED, all elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged. Whether rent was due for the allegedly uninhabitable premises is an issue for a trier of fact. [CONTINUED ON PART TWO] =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YING Q. SUNG VS. GARY NELSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 25, 2021 line 3 (PART TWO). Defendants Demurrer To Complaint (Part Two) 7. Demurrer to the seventh cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege how and when the notification regarding the locks was provided to the landlord. CC 1941.3(b). 8. Demurrer to the eighth cause of action is OVERRULED to the extent the demurrer is based on the SOL grounds for the same reason as the demurrer to the cause of action for CC 1942.4. The Court notes that this code section does not appear to provide for a separate relief and is used merely as a reference for liability under other sections, e.g. CC 1942.4. 9. Demurrer to the 9th cause of action is SUSTAINED. To the extent this cause of action in based in breach of implied warranty of habitability, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend on stature of limitations grounds. To the extent this cause of action is based on some other express or implied covenants, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the specific covenants/provisions and their not time barred breaches. 10. Demurrer to the tenth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend on statute of limitations grounds. 11. Demurer to the sixteenth cause of action is OVERRULED on the sole statute of limitations ground raised in the demurrer. This cause of action is based in part on other causes of action that survived the demurrer on the same ground. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
